displayTitle: The Curve Is Just a Picture Until You Verify It

beats.narration: You drew the Z-curve once to learn its shape. This lab makes it earn its keep — an instrument you verify before you trust.

payload.title: THE CURVE BECOMES AN INSTRUMENT
payload.sub: Prove it against knowns, then read the unknown.

beats.narration: A curve you drew is a picture; a verified curve is an instrument. Re-touch knowns, bracket the range, confirm a mid-range check.

payload.heading: Three checks turn a picture into a tool

tiles.s: Mid-range dot on its certified spot

beats.narration: Now the unknown parks between two verified knowns. Its position along the arc reads conductivity — but spacing isn't linear, so read the curve, not a chord.

payload.heading: Position along the locus is the measurement
payload.note: READ ALONG THE CURVE · NEVER PAST THE LAST KNOWN

beats.narration: And watch the dot lie three ways. Ferromagnetic climbs above the air point. Too thin shifts with the backing. Warm reads low.
Discipline decides whether the number is real. Knowns prove the setup, position reads the metal, and anything failing a check gets re-measured.

payload.sub: A dot is only as honest as its variables.
